---
# The title is intentionally commented out.
#
# In Quarto's Typst format, a YAML title is rendered as a visible title block.
# This CV builds its own custom header below, so leaving `title` active would
# print an unwanted heading before the profile image and header block.
# title: "Your Name CV"

# Render this document with Quarto's Typst engine.
#
# Typst gives us a PDF-first workflow without relying on LaTeX, vitae,
# awesomecv, custom .cls files, or custom .sty files.
format:
  typst:
    # Do not generate a table of contents.
    toc: false

    # Typst uses `us-letter`, not `letter`, for U.S. Letter paper.
    papersize: us-letter

    # Page margins. These are tuned to fit comfortably on U.S. Letter paper.
    # Adjust here if you need more or less whitespace.
    margin:
      x: 0.62in
      y: 0.58in

# Parameters reserved for future CV variants.
#
# These are not yet fully wired into the document, but they provide a place to
# support things like a shorter resume, a rolling year window, or a limited
# number of presentations.
params:
  resume: false
  years: 5
  npresentations: 10

# Global execution defaults for R chunks.
#
# The CV should only show formatted CV content, not source code, package
# messages, or warnings.
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup

# Load the curriculr package.
#
# curriculr provides read_cv_data() and cv_render_section(), as well as the
# internal Typst layout functions that convert data-frame rows into raw Typst
# blocks.
library(curriculr)

# Read the CV workbook.
#
# read_cv_data() reads all sheets from the Excel workbook and returns a named
# list. Each sheet becomes one list element:
#   cv$education
#   cv$experience
#   cv$projects
#   ... and so on
#
# The profile sheet is returned as a named character vector:
#   cv$profile[["first_name"]]
#   cv$profile[["email"]]
#
# Edit the path below if your workbook has a different name or location.
cv <- read_cv_data("data/cv-data-template.xlsx")
```

```{=typst}
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Document style settings
// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
//
// These settings control the global typography and color palette for the CV.
// The page size and margins are controlled in the Quarto YAML front matter
// above. Adjust font, sizes, and colors here to change the overall look.

#set text(font: "Lato", size: 8.8pt, fill: rgb("#3f3f3f"))
#set par(justify: false, leading: 0.48em)

#let accent    = rgb("#c5050c")
#let dark      = rgb("#303030")
#let bodygray  = rgb("#555555")
#let lightgray = rgb("#777777")
#let rulegray  = rgb("#d9d9d9")
```

```{r}
#| label: header
#| results: asis

# Build the CV header block from the profile sheet.
#
# All personal information — name, address, contact details, profile statement,
# and photo path — comes from the `profile` sheet of the Excel workbook.
# Edit the workbook, not this file, to update your personal information.

first_name       <- cv$profile[["first_name"]]
last_name        <- cv$profile[["last_name"]]
address          <- cv$profile[["address"]]
email            <- cv$profile[["email"]]
website          <- cv$profile[["website"]]
github           <- cv$profile[["github"]]
linkedin         <- cv$profile[["linkedin"]]
profile_statement <- cv$profile[["profile_statement"]]
photo            <- cv$profile[["photo"]]

# Escape all profile values for safe use in Typst.
first_name        <- .typst_escape(first_name)
last_name         <- .typst_escape(last_name)
address           <- .typst_escape(address)
email             <- .typst_escape(email)
website           <- .typst_escape(website)
github            <- .typst_escape(github)
linkedin          <- .typst_escape(linkedin)
profile_statement <- .typst_escape(profile_statement)

# The contact line assembles website, email, github, and linkedin.
# Fields that are blank are omitted automatically.
contact_parts <- c(website, email,
                   if (nzchar(github))   paste0("github.com/", github),
                   if (nzchar(linkedin)) paste0("linkedin.com/", linkedin))
contact_parts <- contact_parts[nzchar(contact_parts)]
contact_line  <- paste(contact_parts, collapse = " \u00b7 ")  # middle dot

cat(sprintf(
  paste0(
    '\n```{=typst}\n',
    '#grid(columns: (1.36in, 1fr), gutter: 0.42in,\n',
    '  [#image("%s", width: 1.27in)],\n',
    '  [\n',
    '    #align(center)[\n',
    '      #text(size: 27pt, weight: "light",  fill: rgb("#777777"))[%s]',
    '#h(0.16em)',
    '#text(size: 27pt, weight: "bold", fill: dark)[%s]\n',
    '      #v(0.16em)\n',
    '      #text(size: 8.2pt, fill: lightgray)[%s]\n',
    '      #v(0.18em)\n',
    '      #text(size: 8.3pt, fill: lightgray)[%s]\n',
    '      #v(0.46em)\n',
    '      #text(size: 9pt, style: "italic", fill: bodygray)[%s]\n',
    '    ]\n',
    '  ]\n',
    ')\n',
    '#v(0.85em)\n',
    '```\n'
  ),
  photo, first_name, last_name,
  address, contact_line, profile_statement
))
```

```{r}
#| label: cv-sections
#| results: asis

# The following section renders raw Typst from the Excel workbook.
#
# Each section is built in two steps:
#   1. cat(.cv_section(...))  prints the section heading.
#   2. cv_render_section(...) prints entries row-by-row.
#
# `results = 'asis'` is required because the functions output raw Typst code
# that Quarto should pass through directly into the final document.
#
# To reorder sections, move the corresponding block up or down.
# To remove a section, delete or comment out its block.
# To add a section, follow the pattern established below.

# Education ---------------------------------------------------------------
# Uses year-only date ranges. Maps:
#   title       -> entry title (degree name)
#   institution -> organization line
#   detail      -> secondary description
cat(.cv_section("Education"))
cv_render_section(
  cv$education,
  title_col  = "title",
  org_col    = "institution",
  detail_col = "detail",
  date_fun   = .cv_year_range
)

# Experience --------------------------------------------------------------
# Uses full month/year ranges. Maps:
#   title  -> entry title
#   unit   -> organization line
#   detail -> secondary description
cat(.cv_section("Experience"))
cv_render_section(
  cv$experience,
  title_col  = "title",
  org_col    = "unit",
  detail_col = "detail",
  date_fun   = .cv_date_range
)

# Projects ----------------------------------------------------------------
# Uses full month/year ranges. Maps:
#   title  -> entry title (project name)
#   unit   -> organization line
#   detail -> secondary description
cat(.cv_section("Projects"))
cv_render_section(
  cv$projects,
  title_col  = "title",
  org_col    = "unit",
  detail_col = "detail",
  date_fun   = .cv_date_range
)

# Invited Teaching --------------------------------------------------------
# Uses a custom inline date function: "Month Year" only, no end date.
cat(.cv_section("Invited Teaching"))
cv_render_section(
  cv$invited_teaching,
  title_col = "title",
  org_col   = "unit",
  date_fun  = function(row) {
    trimws(paste(.cv_value(row, "startMonth"), .cv_value(row, "startYear")))
  }
)

# Publications ------------------------------------------------------------
# Uses publication year only. Maps:
#   title -> entry title
#   unit  -> publisher or venue
#   detail -> additional context
cat(.cv_section("Publications"))
cv_render_section(
  cv$publications,
  title_col  = "title",
  org_col    = "unit",
  detail_col = "detail",
  date_fun   = function(row) .cv_value(row, "startYear")
)

# Presentations -----------------------------------------------------------
# The event/venue is the entry title; the presentation title is the secondary
# line. Date is formatted as "Month Year".
cat(.cv_section("Presentations"))
cv_render_section(
  cv$presentations,
  title_col = "unit",
  org_col   = "title",
  date_fun  = function(row) {
    trimws(paste(.cv_value(row, "startMonth"), .cv_value(row, "startYear")))
  }
)

# Certifications ----------------------------------------------------------
# Simple entries: no dates, no location.
cat(.cv_section("Certifications"))
cv_render_section(
  cv$certifications,
  title_col = "title",
  org_col   = "unit",
  date_fun  = NULL,
  where_col = NULL
)

# Grants and Awards -------------------------------------------------------
# Uses full date ranges where available.
cat(.cv_section("Grants and Awards"))
cv_render_section(
  cv$grants_and_awards,
  title_col  = "title",
  org_col    = "unit",
  date_fun   = .cv_date_range
)

# Administrative Services -------------------------------------------------
# Uses full date ranges where available.
cat(.cv_section("Administrative Services"))
cv_render_section(
  cv$admin_services,
  title_col  = "title",
  org_col    = "unit",
  date_fun   = .cv_date_range
)

# Skills ------------------------------------------------------------------
# Simple entries: no dates, no location.
#   title -> skill area
#   unit  -> skill list
cat(.cv_section("Skills"))
cv_render_section(
  cv$skills,
  title_col = "title",
  org_col   = "unit",
  date_fun  = NULL,
  where_col = NULL
)

# Affiliations ------------------------------------------------------------
# Single-column entries: organization name only.
cat(.cv_section("Affiliations"))
cv_render_section(
  cv$affiliations,
  title_col = "title",
  date_fun  = NULL,
  where_col = NULL
)
```
